title: Daily Git and Management Summary Reports
content:
# Daily Git and Management Summary Reports

## Purpose and Scope

The project maintains two complementary daily report formats generated from `jolliai/jolli@main` and `jolliai/jolliai@main`:

- **Git Activity Summary** — a technical audience report covering commits, contributors, files touched, net lines, tickets closed, and detailed per-commit breakdowns.
- **Management Summary** — an executive-audience report translating the same engineering activity into business impact, deployment notes, and risk flags.

Both report types are generated from `git log` over a rolling 24-hour window (or a stated multi-day period for standup summaries).

---

## Git Activity Summary Format

### Standard Sections

| Section | Content |
|---|---|
| **TL;DR** | 3–5 bullet narrative of the day's biggest themes |
| **By the Numbers** | Commits, contributors, files touched, net lines added/removed, tickets closed |
| **What Shipped / Merged** | One row per closed/fixed commit: hash, author, message, what changed |
| **In Progress** | Tickets with "Part of" commits — not yet closed |
| **Worth a Closer Look** | Flagged items: large diffs, new services, CI changes, security-sensitive changes, feature flags |
| **Detailed Breakdown** | Per-contributor tables with file-level detail |

### Metrics Captured Per Report

- Commit count (24 h window)
- Contributor count and names
- Files touched (with overlap note when commits share files)
- Net insertions / deletions
- Ticket references (closed vs. in-progress)
- Timezone spread of contributors

### Risk / Review Flags Used

| Flag | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 🔴 | Massive diff or production-critical change |
| 🟡 | Feature flag gating, DB migration, security/access-control change, bot commit |
| 🟠 | Large deletion of previously load-bearing files |
| ⚠️ | Large diff, low-level infrastructure change, operational risk |
| ℹ️ | Informational (timezone spread, paired commits to review together) |

---

## Management Summary Format

### Standard Sections

| Section | Content |
|---|---|
| **Executive Summary** | 1–2 paragraph narrative of the day's themes in business language |
| **Ticket Highlights** | Per-ticket entries with priority, plain-English description of what was fixed/built, and business impact |
| **Team Activity** | Table of engineer → tickets contributed |
| **Deployment Notes** | Numbered action items: migrations required, feature flags to confirm, IAM/infra changes, breaking-change status |

### Ticket Priority Coding

| Icon | Priority |
|---|---|
| 🔴 | High — production fix or blocking issue |
| 🟡 | Medium — user-facing or internal improvement |
| 🟢 | Low — polish, permission tweak, or minor bug fix |

### Deployment Notes Pattern

Each deployment note entry carries a severity (🔴/🟡/🟢) and specifies:
- Whether a DB migration is included and what it does
- Whether new IAM/cloud permissions are required
- Whether a feature flag must be confirmed per environment
- Whether any breaking changes exist for end users

---

## Standup / Multi-Day Summary Format

For periods covering more than one day (e.g., two-week sprint summaries), reports use a standup format with:

- **By Contributor** — chronological list of each engineer's commits with dates
- **By Theme / Area** — grouped narrative sections (e.g., Force-Push Safety, IntelliJ UI Overhaul, Knowledge Graph, MCP Tools, Telemetry)
- **Dependency Bumps** — table of automated Dependabot upgrades separated from application changes

---

## Recurring Structural Patterns

### Commit Message Convention
Commits follow `<Closes|Fixes|Part of> JOLLI-NNNN: <imperative description>`. "Part of" signals in-progress work; "Closes" or "Fixes" signals fully merged work.

### Contributor Timezone Spread
Reports note UTC offset for each contributor. The core team spans UTC+8 (Flyer Li, Sam.Li, finn.zhong, foster.han) and UTC−7 (douglas.schroeder), producing a ~15-hour spread with narrow overlap windows. Reports flag this when shared files (e.g., `WorkflowRouter`, `SiteRouter`) are touched by both timezone groups in the same window.

### Bot Commits
`jolli-bot` authors automated commits (e.g., marketing-site redirects). Reports flag bot commits for intentionality confirmation.

### Large-Diff Threshold
Commits touching 40+ files or adding 3,000+ lines are flagged for dedicated review passes regardless of ticket priority.

### Feature Flag Discipline
New infrastructure paths (e.g., `PER_PROVIDER_POOL_ENABLED` for per-provider DB connection pooling) are gated behind named flags. Reports call out flag state verification per environment as a deployment action item.

---

## Repositories Covered

| Repo | Content |
|---|---|
| `jolliai/jolli` | Backend, frontend, CLI (`space-cli`), infrastructure, DB migrations, CI pipelines |
| `jolliai/jolliai` | IDE clients (VS Code, IntelliJ), CLI client tooling, knowledge graph, MCP server |

Reports are generated separately per repo and cross-referenced when changes in one affect the other (e.g., CLI releases in `jolliai/jolliai` consuming backend APIs from `jolliai/jolli`).

---

## Known Active Work Tracks (as of latest reports)

The following tickets appear across multiple report windows as long-running tracks:

| Ticket | Description | Last Status |
|---|---|---|
| JOLLI-1777 | Unified git-backed spaces (jolli-git + GitHub-backed merged) | Closed |
| JOLLI-1830/1831 | Per-provider PostgreSQL connection pooling | Closed |
| JOLLI-1843 | Workflow job cancellation | Closed |
| JOLLI-1785 | Anonymous opt-out telemetry (CLI, VS Code, IntelliJ) | Closed |
| JOLLI-1618 | AI-powered auto-merge with hunk-based conflict resolution | In progress |
| JOLLI-1571 | Production EFS vault mount reliability | In progress (fixes landed) |
| JOLLI-1734 | Path-addressed structural ops for git-backed spaces | In progress |
| JOLLI-1731 | AWS Lambda relay + Claude Code Action automation | In progress |
| Knowledge Base compilation | Topic-centric KB architecture, `jolli compile` command, multi-repo ingest | In progress (SP3–SP6) |
| MCP server / full-text search | Orama index over KB + commit catalog, 4 MCP tools via stdio | Shipped |

---
